contest.title: 2024-2025 ICPC, NERC, Northern Eurasia Finals (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Read the operation semantics and constraints: n,m<=100000, the initial register is false, the input DAG specifies precedence, and at most two operations returned true.
Official tutorial compresses the register lifetime into false/true/false phases and reduces feasibility to a topological ordering with phase constraints.
Inspected an accepted contest implementation using phase-aware zero-indegree queues for the four operation/result classes.

Proved that eagerly consuming a currently legal zero-indegree no-change operation cannot hurt. Differential-tested the phase-aware Kahn algorithm against exhaustive topological-order simulation on 28,000 random DAG instances with n<=7.

reference_candidates.notes: Luogu solution index candidate; unavailable during this review and therefore not used as evidence.

annotation.summary: Find a linear extension of a DAG of set/unset operations whose observed boolean return values are consistent with executing the operations from an initially false register.
annotation.constraints: n,m<=100000 and at most two operations returned true, requiring an O(n+m) graph algorithm.
annotation.core_idea: A true result changes the register, so at most two true results imply at most three phases: false, then possibly true, then possibly false. In a false phase, only unset-false operations preserve the state; in a true phase, only set-false operations preserve it. Set-true and unset-true are the phase transitions. First validate that the true-result operations form one of the possible transition patterns. Run Kahn's algorithm with ready vertices separated by operation class: drain all currently legal no-change operations, require the next transition to have zero indegree, switch phase, and continue. Remaining unset-false operations may naturally fall before the first transition or after the second according to the DAG.
tricks: Compress the entire register history into at most three phases.
Classify vertices by both operation type and reported result.
Maintain separate zero-indegree queues so only operations legal in the current phase are emitted.
Handle zero, one, or two true-result operations explicitly and reject impossible transition patterns.

solution_variants.name: phase-aware-kahn-toposort
solution_variants.summary: Use four ready queues for unset-false, set-false, set-true, and unset-true. Drain the legal no-change queue for each register phase and emit a required transition only when it is ready. A solution exists exactly when all vertices are emitted.

tags.description: Use a topological ordering, usually constructed with indegrees and Kahn's algorithm, as the central construction or feasibility test; exclude DAG problems where topological ordering is only incidental.
tags.created_reason: The existing graph/dag tag is too broad for retrieving problems whose decisive operation is constructing a constrained topological order.
tags.evidence: The required serialization is exactly a topological ordering, with zero-indegree choices additionally restricted by the current register phase.
The bound of two true results compresses an arbitrary-length execution into at most three register-state phases.
All given precedence constraints form a DAG and are maintained through indegree updates.
Every currently legal zero-indegree no-change operation can be emitted immediately without reducing future feasibility.
